paper_title: Growth Rate Calculation and Lipid Droplet Measurements

methods.description: Process for calculating the growth rate and doubling time of selected diatom strains using cell counts over a period of 13 days.

steps.description: Five strains were chosen: S. trachyderma (SZCZ E1420, E1421, E1427, and E1768) and S. rawaii (SZCZ E1425).
steps.searchable_summary: select diatom strains for growth rate calculation

steps.description: Cultures were maintained in the exponential growth phase.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain cultures in exponential growth phase

steps.description: 9 ml of each diatom strain was inoculated as three replicates into a 250 ml autoclaved Erlenmeyer flask with 80 ml of f/2 medium.
steps.searchable_summary: inoculate diatom strains into flasks with f/2 medium

steps.description: Batch cultures were kept in a growth chamber with PAR of 100 µmol photons m −2 s −1 at 20°C and 12 h photoperiod.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate cultures in growth chamber with specified conditions

steps.description: Cells were counted every second day using the Malassez counting chamber for 13 days.
steps.searchable_summary: count cells using Malassez counting chamber

steps.description: Growth rate and doubling time were calculated following the methods described by Wood et al. (2005).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate growth rate and doubling time

methods.description: Procedure for measuring lipid droplets in diatom strains using flow cytometry and confocal laser scanning microscopy.

steps.description: Cells were stained with Nile Red following the method suggested by Greenspan et al. (1985).
Each strain was divided into batch cultures in standard f/2 medium with modified nitrogen levels in triplicates.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare batch cultures with modified nitrogen levels

steps.description: Cell abundance, Nile Red fluorescence, and cell size were measured using a CytoFLEX flow cytometer.
steps.searchable_summary: measure cell abundance, fluorescence, and size using flow cytometer

steps.description: Samples were strained on a 35 µm Nylon mesh to remove most chains of cells.
steps.searchable_summary: strain samples to remove cell chains

steps.description: Polystyrene microspheres were added to each sample as an internal standard.
steps.searchable_summary: add polystyrene microspheres as internal standard

steps.description: Nile Red stained samples were observed using an LSM700 laser scanning confocal microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: observe stained samples with confocal microscope

steps.description: The lipid droplets in cells were measured and compared with flow cytometry results.
steps.searchable_summary: measure lipid droplets and compare with flow cytometry
